default_creds.context: settings.yaml server.auth.secret base64-encodes secret:key as the EXAMPLE bearer token; inert because server.auth.enabled is false by default. Only live if an operator enables auth without changing the secret.

install_tell: Classic (dominant population): git clone https://github.com/zylon-ai/private-gpt && poetry install --extras "ui llms-ollama embeddings-ollama vector-stores-qdrant" && PGPT_PROFILES=ollama make run  -> Gradio UI + API on http://localhost:8001 | 1.0 rewrite: uv tool install --python 3.11 --find-links https://wheels.privategpt.dev/packages/ "private-gpt[core]" && OPENAI_API_BASE=http://localhost:<port>/v1 private-gpt serve  -> http://localhost:8080/ui
misconfig_patterns: settings.yaml server.auth.enabled=false -> every FastAPI route unauth (auth.py installs always-true authenticated())
Gradio UI mounted at / (classic :8001) or /ui (1.0 :8080) with no login when auth disabled
docker-compose / make run binds 0.0.0.0:8001 -> exposing the host puts the whole RAG API on the internet
Ingested corpus listable (/v1/ingest/list) and retrievable (/v1/chunks) without credentials
CVE-2024-5186 SSRF via ingest path parameter reaches cloud metadata on 0.5.0

response_markers: Gradio config blob in HTML root with title containing 'PrivateGPT'
FastAPI {"detail":...} body on a malformed GET /v1/ingest/list
active_probe.false_positive_check: GET /health -> {"status":"ok"} is generic and collides with any FastAPI app. Disambiguate PrivateGPT by requiring TWO vendor-unique signals together: (1) the Gradio root HTML whose <title>/config JSON contains 'PrivateGPT', AND (2) the vendor-unique routes /v1/chunks and /v1/ingest/list (a bare /v1/chat/completions is NOT unique — LocalAI, vLLM, LM Studio, Ollama all answer the OpenAI-compatible /v1). Never fingerprint on the substrings 'chat', 'GPT', or 'AI' alone (50%-rule false positives against thousands of unrelated chatbots and Open WebUI). Note two version eras: classic = :8001 + Gradio at /; 1.0 rewrite = :8080 + UI at /ui — title still 'PrivateGPT'.

deployment_tells: Port 8001 + Gradio UI at / = classic zylon-ai/private-gpt (dominant deployed population)
Port 8080 + UI at /ui + Anthropic-API framing = 1.0 rewrite
PGPT_PROFILES env (ollama|docker|local|mock) in docker-compose reveals inference backend; 'mock' profile UI echoes input
Default vector store Qdrant on :6333 colocated
pivot_paths: GET / (Gradio) -> upload/query UI with no login when auth disabled
GET /v1/ingest/list -> enumerate ingested document filenames/doc_ids (reveals loaded data: contracts, PII, source)
POST /v1/chunks {text} -> retrieve verbatim chunks from ingested docs unauth (RAG corpus read)
POST /v1/chat/completions use_context=true -> exfiltrate document content via grounded answers
settings.yaml on host (if filesystem reachable) -> upstream OPENAI_API_KEY / provider creds
vulnerabilities: CVE-2024-5186 (CVSS 8.3 HIGH, CWE-918 SSRF): the file-upload 'path' parameter in privategpt 0.5.0 lets an attacker drive arbitrary server-side requests, including the AWS metadata endpoint; reported via Huntr against imartinez/zylon-ai privategpt. Affected: 0.5.0.

Design exposure (not a CVE, primary-source confirmed): server.auth.enabled defaults to false, so any default internet-exposed deployment serves the full RAG API + Gradio UI with no authentication. Affects: all versions in default config.
auth_evidence: Primary source: settings.yaml in zylon-ai/private-gpt ships `server.auth.enabled: false` (server block: `port: ${PORT:8080}`, `auth:\n  enabled: false\n  secret: "Basic c2VjcmV0OmtleQ=="`). The companion source private_gpt/server/utils/auth.py proves the consequence: `if not settings().server.auth.enabled:` it defines `def authenticated(): return True` — a dummy dependency that always authenticates every FastAPI route. The ingested-doc, /v1/chunks retrieval, and chat endpoints are therefore fully unauth in a stock deploy. The `secret:key` base64 token in settings.yaml is only the EXAMPLE token used IF an operator flips enabled to true; it is inert by default. The classic quickstart (`PGPT_PROFILES=ollama make run`) and docker-compose bind 0.0.0.0:8001 and serve the Gradio UI + API with no login. Auth code exists and is OFF unless `server.auth.enabled: true` is set -> configurable-default-off, not open-by-default.
sensitive_assets: Ingested RAG documents and PII: filenames/doc_ids via GET /v1/ingest/list, verbatim source text via POST /v1/chunks
Upstream LLM provider API key (OPENAI_API_KEY / Azure / provider creds) in settings.yaml or env
Conversation content via the unauth POST /v1/chat/completions and /v1/completions endpoints
Colocated vector store contents (default Qdrant :6333, or Chroma/PGVector) if separately exposed
Cloud IAM credentials via CVE-2024-5186 SSRF reaching 169.254.169.254 metadata (0.5.0)
sources: [CANDIDATE - NuClide Cat-34 rag-app-builders Stage -1 OSINT 2026-06-12; software-default characterization, no live host probed yet]
